text: AeroBrew Coffee Maker - User Manual Model: CM-410 Category: Kitchen MSRP: $79 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 12-cup carafe - 1000W heating element - programmable 24-hour timer - auto shutoff after 2 hours 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Rinse the carafe and filter basket before first use. 2. Fill the reservoir with cold water up to the 12-cup line. 3. Insert a paper filter or the reusable mesh filter into the basket. 4. Add ground coffee (1 tbsp per cup is recommended) and close the lid. 5. Press the Power button, then Brew to start. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Water reservoir is empty or not seated correctly. - E2: Heating element overheated; unplug and let cool for 30 minutes. - E4: Carafe not detected on the warming plate. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (CM-410) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not operate with a cracked or chipped carafe. - Unplug before cleaning; never immerse the base in water. - Keep the steam vent clear of obstructions while brewing. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. Extended to 3 years if registered online within 30 days of purchase. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Descale every 60 brew cycles using a 1:1 water-vinegar solution. - Wash the mesh filter after every use. - Wipe the warming plate with a damp cloth weekly.

BrightView 27-inch Monitor - User Manual Model: MN-27Q Category: Electronics MSRP: $249 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 2560x1440 resolution - 165Hz refresh rate - 1ms response time - HDR400 support 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Attach the stand to the monitor base before mounting to the panel. 2. Connect via DisplayPort for full 165Hz refresh rate (HDMI caps at 144Hz). 3. Use the joystick on the rear panel to access the on-screen menu. 4. Enable HDR in your operating system's display settings. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: No signal detected; check cable connection and input source. - E2: Refresh rate not supported by current cable; switch to DisplayPort. - E5: Panel self-test failure; contact support. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (MN-27Q) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not press directly on the LCD panel. - Use a surge protector; power spikes can damage the internal board. - Allow ventilation space above and behind the monitor. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 3-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 3-year warranty covering dead pixels beyond the industry-standard threshold. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Dust the vents on the rear panel monthly. - Use a microfiber cloth only; avoid ammonia-based cleaners on the screen. - Recalibrate color settings every few months for accuracy.

ChopMaster Food Processor - User Manual Model: FP-260 Category: Kitchen MSRP: $149 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 9-cup work bowl - 3 interchangeable blades - 700W motor - pulse and continuous modes 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Wash all detachable parts before first use. 2. Attach the work bowl to the base and lock the lid. 3. Insert the desired blade attachment onto the drive shaft. 4. Use the pulse button for chopping, continuous mode for purees. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Lid not locked; the motor will not start. - E3: Bowl overfilled past the max fill line. - E4: Motor thermal cutoff triggered; wait 15 minutes before restarting. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (FP-260) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Handle blades by the plastic hub, never the cutting edge. - Do not process ingredients hotter than 175°F. - Unplug before disassembling the blade attachment. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year limited warranty on the motor base; blades are not covered. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Hand wash blades immediately after use to prevent dulling. - Check the lid gasket for cracks every few months. - Store blades in the included protective case.

CleanCycle Washing Machine - User Manual Model: WM-800 Category: Home MSRP: $649 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 4.5 cu ft capacity - 12 wash cycles - 1200 RPM max spin speed - ENERGY STAR certified 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Remove shipping bolts from the rear panel before first use. 2. Level the machine using the adjustable feet; check with a bubble level. 3. Connect hot and cold water hoses to the correct valves. 4. Run an empty calibration cycle before washing your first load. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Door not latched; the machine will not start a cycle. - E2: Water supply valve closed or hose kinked. - E4: Unbalanced load detected during spin cycle. - E8: Drain hose blocked or improperly positioned. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (WM-800) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Leaving shipping bolts installed can cause serious vibration damage; remove before use. - Do not overload past the max fill line for your selected cycle. - Turn off the water supply if leaving home for an extended period. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 3-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 3-year warranty; drum and motor covered for 10 years under normal residential use. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Run a monthly cleaning cycle with washing machine cleaner. - Leave the door ajar between uses to prevent mold and odor. - Clean the detergent drawer every few weeks.

ClearSound Wireless Earbuds - User Manual Model: WE-33 Category: Electronics MSRP: $59 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - Bluetooth 5.3 - 24-hour total battery with case - active noise cancellation - IPX4 sweat resistance 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Charge the case fully via USB-C before first use (about 90 minutes). 2. Remove both earbuds from the case to enter pairing mode automatically. 3. Select the earbuds from your device's Bluetooth menu. 4. Use the companion app to customize the touch controls. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Left and right earbuds out of sync; return both to the case for 10 seconds. - E2: Case battery below 5%; charging required before use. - E3: Firmware update failed; keep earbuds within 3 feet of your phone and retry. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (WE-33) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not submerge in water; IPX4 only protects against sweat and light rain. - Keep volume below 85 decibels for extended listening sessions. - Discontinue use if you experience ear irritation. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 1-year warranty; excludes damage from moisture beyond IPX4 rating. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Clean the ear tips weekly with a dry, soft cloth. - Avoid leaving the case in direct sunlight or hot cars. - Replace ear tips every 6 months for the best seal.

CrispAir Toaster Oven - User Manual Model: TO-330 Category: Kitchen MSRP: $99 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 0.6 cu ft interior - 6 cooking functions - 60-minute timer with auto shutoff - fits a 9-inch pizza 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Remove all packaging materials from inside the oven. 2. Run the oven empty at 400°F for 10 minutes to burn off manufacturing residue. 3. Select a cooking function using the dial. 4. Set temperature and time, then press Start. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Door not fully closed. - E2: Internal temperature sensor fault. - E7: Fan motor obstruction detected. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (TO-330) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Exterior surfaces become hot during use; use oven mitts. - Do not line the crumb tray with foil. - Keep at least 4 inches of clearance from walls and cabinets. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. Standard 2-year warranty; heating elements covered for 3 years. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Empty the crumb tray after every 3-4 uses. - Wipe the interior with a damp cloth once cool. - Check the door seal yearly for gaps.

EcoLock Smart Deadbolt - User Manual Model: DL-210 Category: Smart Home MSRP: $179 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - fingerprint, code, and app unlock - auto-lock after 30 seconds - battery backup (4 AA, 6-month life) - fits standard door prep 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Verify your door thickness (1-3/8 to 2 inches) matches compatibility before installing. 2. Remove the existing deadbolt and retain mounting screws if compatible. 3. Install the exterior and interior assemblies, connecting the wiring harness between them. 4. Insert batteries and complete setup through the companion app. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Low battery warning; replace all 4 AA batteries as a set. - E2: Fingerprint not recognized after 3 attempts; re-enroll in the app. - E4: Door misaligned; deadbolt cannot extend fully into the strike plate. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (DL-210) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Keep a physical backup key accessible in case of full battery failure. - Do not use rechargeable batteries; voltage drop can cause malfunctions. - Test the lock thoroughly before relying on it as your primary door lock. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty on electronics; mechanical deadbolt components covered for 5 years. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Replace all 4 batteries together every 6 months, not individually. - Lubricate the bolt mechanism yearly with graphite lubricant. - Wipe the fingerprint sensor with a dry cloth if recognition slows down.

EmberGlow Camp Stove - User Manual Model: CS-100 Category: Outdoor MSRP: $69 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 2-burner design - 10,000 BTU per burner - piezo ignition - folds to 16x4x14 inches 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Attach the propane canister to the regulator hose before igniting. 2. Check all connections for gas leaks using soapy water before first use. 3. Turn the burner knob and press the piezo igniter simultaneously. 4. Allow the stove to cool completely before folding for storage. 3. SAFETY WARNINGS - Never use inside a tent or enclosed space; carbon monoxide risk. - Keep at least 24 inches from flammable materials. - Check for gas leaks before every use, not just the first. 4. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty on burner components; regulator hose covered for 1 year. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 5. MAINTENANCE - Clean burner ports with a pin if flame is uneven. - Store the propane canister separately in a cool, dry place. - Inspect the hose for cracks each season.

FreshBrew Electric Kettle - User Manual Model: EK-170 Category: Kitchen MSRP: $44 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 1.7L capacity - 1500W rapid boil - 5 temperature presets - keep-warm mode for 30 minutes 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Rinse the interior with water before first use; discard the first boil. 2. Fill to the desired level between the min and max lines. 3. Select a temperature preset for tea, coffee, or general boiling. 4. Press the keep-warm button if you want water held at temperature. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Kettle not seated correctly on the power base. - E2: Dry-boil protection triggered; add water before continuing. - E4: Temperature sensor fault; contact support if error persists. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (EK-170) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not fill above the max line to avoid boiling water splashing out. - Do not operate without the lid closed. - Descale regularly to prevent heating element damage. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 1-year warranty; excludes mineral scale buildup damage. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Descale monthly in hard water areas using a vinegar solution. - Wipe the exterior with a soft, dry cloth. - Check the power base contacts for moisture before each use.

FrostPeak Mini Fridge - User Manual Model: MF-210 Category: Kitchen MSRP: $189 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 3.2 cu ft capacity - reversible door - adjustable thermostat (34-45°F) - quiet compressor (under 40dB) 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Let the unit stand upright for 4 hours before plugging in. 2. Place on a level, ventilated surface with 3 inches of clearance on all sides. 3. Set the thermostat dial to level 3 for normal use. 4. Allow 24 hours to reach set temperature before loading with food. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Door left open for more than 5 minutes. - E3: Compressor fault; contact support if error persists after restart. - E5: Ambient temperature too high for normal operation (above 100°F). If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (MF-210) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not block the rear ventilation grille. - Do not store flammable items or aerosol cans inside. - Disconnect power before moving the unit. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. Compressor covered separately for 5 years; all other parts 1 year. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Defrost manually every 3-6 months if frost exceeds 1/4 inch. - Clean the door gasket monthly with mild soap. - Vacuum the rear coils twice a year.

GreenGrow Garden Tiller - User Manual Model: GT-140 Category: Outdoor MSRP: $219 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 40V battery powered - 10-inch tilling width - 6-inch tilling depth - foldable handle for storage 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Charge the battery fully before first use (approx. 2 hours). 2. Attach the tines by aligning the mounting holes and inserting the pins. 3. Adjust the handle height to a comfortable working position. 4. Start on a low-depth setting for compacted or unfamiliar soil. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Battery not seated correctly in the compartment. - E3: Tines jammed with roots or debris; power off before clearing. - E5: Motor overload; reduce tilling depth and retry. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (GT-140) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Wear sturdy closed-toe shoes and eye protection while operating. - Clear the work area of rocks and debris before starting. - Power off and remove the battery before clearing jams. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty on the motor; battery warranty is 1 year separately. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Clean soil and plant debris from the tines after every use. - Store the battery indoors in moderate temperatures. - Sharpen or replace tines annually for best performance.

GuardianEye Security Camera - User Manual Model: SC-360 Category: Smart Home MSRP: $99 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 1080p HD video - night vision up to 30 feet - two-way audio - local and cloud storage options 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Download the companion app and create an account before unboxing. 2. Mount the camera bracket using the included screws and anchors. 3. Power on and scan the QR code on the camera base with the app. 4. Select local (microSD) or cloud storage in the app settings. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Camera offline; check Wi-Fi signal strength at the mounting location. - E3: microSD card not detected or improperly formatted. - E6: Motion detection sensitivity misconfigured, causing frequent false alerts. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (SC-360) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not mount facing directly into strong sunlight; it can wash out footage. - Ensure mounting hardware is rated for outdoor use if installing outside. - Review local laws regarding audio recording before enabling two-way audio. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty on hardware; cloud storage subscription is billed separately. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Wipe the lens with a microfiber cloth monthly. - Check mounting screws for looseness every few months. - Reformat the microSD card every 6 months to maintain write speed.

HomeSense Smart Thermostat - User Manual Model: ST-900 Category: Smart Home MSRP: $149 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - Wi-Fi enabled - learns schedule after 1 week - compatible with most 24V HVAC systems - geofencing support 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Turn off power to your HVAC system at the breaker before installation. 2. Label existing wires before disconnecting your old thermostat. 3. Mount the new base plate and connect wires per the included wiring guide. 4. Restore power and follow the app's setup wizard to connect Wi-Fi. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Wiring configuration not recognized; verify against the wiring guide. - E2: Wi-Fi connection lost; check router placement and signal. - E4: HVAC system not responding; check breaker and wire connections. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (ST-900) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Turn off power at the breaker before any wiring work. - Do not install near direct sunlight or heat vents, which skews readings. - Consult an HVAC professional if your system uses a C-wire alternative. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 3-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 3-year warranty; professional installation costs are not covered. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Clean the display with a dry microfiber cloth. - Update firmware when prompted in the app. - Replace HVAC filters per your system's schedule for accurate readings.

ImpactForce Driver - User Manual Model: ID-450 Category: Power Tools MSRP: $119 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 18V battery platform - up to 1800 in-lbs torque - 3-speed settings - 1/4-inch hex chuck 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Charge the included battery for at least 60 minutes before first use. 2. Slide the battery into the driver base until it clicks. 3. Select a speed setting appropriate for the fastener size. 4. Insert the driver bit fully into the quick-release chuck. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E2: Battery contacts dirty; clean with a dry cloth. - E3: Overload detected; ease up on applied pressure. - E5: Chuck jammed; remove bit and inspect for debris. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (ID-450) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Wear hearing protection; impact mechanism is loud under load. - Do not use damaged or worn driver bits. - Keep the tool pointed away from yourself and others when testing the trigger. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 3-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 3-year warranty matching the PowerDrive drill's battery platform. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Wipe down the chuck weekly to prevent bit slippage. - Store in the provided case to protect against dust. - Check battery contacts for corrosion every few months.

IronGrip Adjustable Dumbbell Set - User Manual Model: DB-552 Category: Fitness MSRP: $299 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 5-52.5 lb adjustable range per dumbbell - quick-turn dial selector - compact storage tray included - sold as a pair 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Place both dumbbells in the storage tray before adjusting weight. 2. Turn the selector dial to your desired weight increment. 3. Lift straight up and out of the tray to engage only the selected plates. 4. Return to the tray fully before changing the weight setting. 3. SAFETY WARNINGS - Always adjust weight with the dumbbell resting in the tray, not in hand. - Keep fingers clear of the plate selection mechanism. - Use on a flat, stable surface to prevent tipping. 4. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty against mechanical selector failure. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 5. MAINTENANCE - Wipe down the plates after sweaty sessions to prevent rust. - Check the dial mechanism periodically for smooth operation. - Store in the tray, not stacked loosely, to protect the selector pins.

LumaHome Smart Bulb 4-Pack - User Manual Model: SB-4PK Category: Smart Home MSRP: $44 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 800 lumens per bulb - 16 million colors - Wi-Fi direct, no hub required - voice assistant compatible 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Screw bulbs into standard E26 sockets and power on. 2. Open the companion app and select 'Add Device'. 3. Follow the in-app prompts to connect each bulb to Wi-Fi individually. 4. Group bulbs into rooms within the app for scene control. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Bulb not discovered during setup; move closer to the router and retry. - E2: Bulb flickering; check for a compatible dimmer switch (standard dimmers are not supported). - E5: Voice assistant link expired; re-link the skill/action in its app. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (SB-4PK) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not use with incompatible mechanical dimmer switches. - Do not exceed the fixture's rated wattage equivalent. - Allow the bulb to cool before handling after extended use. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 1-year warranty; excludes bulbs used with unsupported dimmer switches. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Dust the bulb surface occasionally to maintain brightness. - Keep firmware updated via the app for new features. - Avoid frequent power cycling, which can shorten bulb lifespan.

NightSky Camping Lantern - User Manual Model: LT-75 Category: Outdoor MSRP: $29 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 300-lumen LED - USB-C rechargeable, 20-hour runtime - IPX6 water resistance - collapsible design 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Charge fully via USB-C before first use (about 3 hours). 2. Press and hold the power button for 2 seconds to turn on. 3. Cycle through brightness levels with a single press. 4. Collapse the lantern body for compact storage when not lighting an area. 3. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not look directly into the LED at close range on high brightness. - Do not charge while wet; dry the USB-C port fully first. - Avoid prolonged storage at full charge; store around 50% for battery health. 4. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 1-year warranty; battery capacity loss after heavy use is not covered. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 5. MAINTENANCE - Wipe the housing with a damp cloth after dusty trips. - Recharge every few months during long storage periods. - Check the USB-C port cover seal periodically.

PowerDrive 20V Drill - User Manual Model: PD-720 Category: Power Tools MSRP: $159 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 20V lithium-ion battery - two-speed gearbox (0-500 / 0-1800 RPM) - 1/2-inch keyless chuck - LED work light 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Charge the battery fully (approx. 90 minutes) before first use. 2. Insert the battery into the drill until it clicks into place. 3. Select forward or reverse rotation with the direction switch. 4. Adjust the clutch collar to the appropriate torque setting for your material. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Battery not fully seated in the drill housing. - E2: Battery below 10% charge; low-power mode engaged. - E5: Motor overheated; release the trigger and let cool for 10 minutes. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (PD-720) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Always wear eye protection when drilling. - Disconnect the battery before changing bits. - Do not use in wet conditions unless rated for it. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 3-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 3-year tool warranty; battery warranty is 2 years. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Clean dust from the chuck jaws after each use. - Store the battery at partial charge if unused for over a month. - Lubricate the chuck mechanism every 6 months.

PulsePro Fitness Watch - User Manual Model: FW-88 Category: Electronics MSRP: $199 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 1.4-inch AMOLED display - 7-day battery life - 5 ATM water resistance - built-in GPS 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Charge fully using the included magnetic charging cable before first use. 2. Download the companion app and create an account. 3. Pair the watch via Bluetooth from the app's device setup screen. 4. Set your profile (height, weight, age) for accurate calorie tracking. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E01: Bluetooth pairing timeout; restart both devices and retry. - E04: GPS signal not found; move to an open outdoor area. - E09: Battery critically low; connect the charging cable. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (FW-88) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Not rated for high-velocity water activities like water skiing. - Avoid charging in temperatures above 95°F. - Consult a doctor before relying on heart-rate alerts for medical decisions. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 1-year limited warranty; battery capacity loss after 12 months is not covered. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Clean the sensor contacts on the back weekly. - Update firmware monthly via the companion app. - Avoid prolonged direct sunlight exposure to prevent display degradation.

PureAir HEPA Purifier - User Manual Model: AP-450 Category: Home MSRP: $159 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - covers up to 450 sq ft - true HEPA H13 filter - 3 fan speeds plus auto mode - air quality sensor 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Remove the plastic wrap from the filter before first use. 2. Place on a flat surface with at least 12 inches of clearance on all sides. 3. Plug in and press the power button; auto mode is recommended for daily use. 4. Check the filter indicator light monthly. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Filter not installed correctly; the unit will not power on. - E2: Filter replacement indicator active; filter needs replacing. - E3: Air quality sensor blocked; clean the sensor intake. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (AP-450) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not operate without a filter installed. - Keep away from water sources; this is not a waterproof appliance. - Do not block the air intake or outlet grilles. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty on the motor; filters are consumable and not covered. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Replace the HEPA filter every 6-8 months under normal use. - Vacuum the pre-filter screen every 2 weeks. - Wipe the air quality sensor with a dry cotton swab monthly.

QuietRun Shop Vac - User Manual Model: SV-600 Category: Power Tools MSRP: $89 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 6-gallon tank - 4.5 peak HP motor - wet/dry capability - washable foam filter 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Attach the desired filter based on wet or dry use. 2. Connect the hose to the inlet port until it clicks. 3. Plug in and use the power switch on the motor head. 4. Empty and rinse the tank after wet pickup before storing. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Tank lid not sealed correctly; suction reduced. - E3: Filter clogged; suction power will drop noticeably. - E4: Float shutoff triggered during wet pickup; empty the tank. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (SV-600) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Never vacuum flammable liquids or hot ashes. - Do not run without a filter installed for dry debris. - Unplug before removing the motor head. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty on the motor; filters and hoses are consumables. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Rinse the foam filter after each wet-pickup use. - Check the float ball for debris that could jam it. - Store the hose uncoiled to prevent kinking.

RidgeLine Hiking Boots - User Manual Model: HB-500 Category: Outdoor MSRP: $139 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - waterproof leather upper - Vibram outsole - mid-ankle support - available in sizes 7-13 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Break in the boots on short walks before long hikes. 2. Use the included insoles or swap for orthotics if needed. 3. Lace snugly at the ankle for support on uneven terrain. 4. Apply waterproofing treatment before first extended use. 3. SAFETY WARNINGS - Not rated for technical mountaineering or ice climbing. - Allow boots to fully dry between uses to prevent material breakdown. - Replace laces if fraying is visible to avoid mid-hike failure. 4. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 1-year warranty against sole separation and stitching defects. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 5. MAINTENANCE - Clean mud off after each hike with a soft brush. - Re-apply waterproofing spray every 2-3 months of regular use. - Store away from direct heat, which can crack the leather.

SilentNight Robot Vacuum - User Manual Model: RV-700 Category: Home MSRP: $299 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - LiDAR mapping navigation - 2700Pa suction - self-emptying base - runs up to 150 minutes per charge 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Place the charging base against a wall with 1.5 feet of clearance on each side. 2. Let the robot fully charge before its first cleaning run. 3. Use the app to run an initial mapping pass of your home. 4. Set up no-go zones in the app for areas to avoid. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Wheels stuck or lifted off the ground; place robot on a flat surface. - E2: Dustbin full; empty it or return to base for auto-empty. - E5: Side brush tangled with hair or debris; clean and reinstall. - E9: LiDAR sensor obstructed; wipe the sensor window. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (RV-700) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Keep cords and small objects off the floor to prevent tangling. - Do not use on wet floors or to pick up liquids. - Supervise use around stairs even though a drop sensor is included. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 1-year warranty; brushes and filters are consumable parts not covered. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Empty and rinse the dustbin weekly. - Clean the side brushes and rollers of hair every 1-2 weeks. - Replace the HEPA filter every 3 months.

SteadyCut Circular Saw - User Manual Model: CS-185 Category: Power Tools MSRP: $139 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 7-1/4 inch blade - 15A corded motor - bevel cuts up to 56 degrees - laser guide 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Inspect the blade guard for free movement before use. 2. Set the cutting depth to roughly 1/4 inch deeper than material thickness. 3. Align the laser guide with your cut line. 4. Allow the blade to reach full speed before contacting material. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Blade guard obstructed; will not retract. - E4: Motor brush wear detected; service recommended. - E6: Laser guide battery low. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (CS-185) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Never remove or wedge open the blade guard. - Keep hands at least 6 inches from the blade path. - Disconnect power before changing blades. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty; does not cover blade wear or laser calibration. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Replace the blade after roughly 500 linear feet of cutting. - Check the bevel adjustment lock monthly. - Clear sawdust from vents after every use.

StreamCast 4K Media Player - User Manual Model: MP-4K Category: Electronics MSRP: $49 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 4K HDR streaming - dual-band Wi-Fi - voice remote - 8GB internal storage 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Connect the HDMI cable to an open port on your TV. 2. Plug in the power adapter and select the correct HDMI input. 3. Follow the on-screen setup wizard to connect to Wi-Fi. 4. Sign in to your streaming accounts through the Settings menu. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Wi-Fi connection dropped; check router distance and signal strength. - E3: Remote not pairing; hold the pair button for 5 seconds. - E7: Storage full; clear cached app data in Settings. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (MP-4K) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not block ventilation slots on the device housing. - Use only the included power adapter. - Keep away from direct heat sources. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 1-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 1-year warranty; does not cover third-party app subscription issues. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Restart the device weekly to clear background processes. - Update system software when prompted for security patches. - Keep the device in a well-ventilated area to avoid overheating.

SummitPeak 2-Person Tent - User Manual Model: TN-220 Category: Outdoor MSRP: $159 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 2-person capacity - 3-season rating - 5.5 lb packed weight - freestanding aluminum poles 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Lay out the footprint before assembling poles. 2. Assemble the two aluminum poles and cross them at the center. 3. Clip the tent body to the poles, starting from the corners. 4. Stake out the rainfly and guy lines for wind stability. 3. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not use as the sole shelter in severe weather; it is rated 3-season only. - Avoid pitching under dead trees or branches. - Seal seams before first use in wet conditions. 4. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty against pole breakage and fabric defects. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 5. MAINTENANCE - Dry the tent completely before packing away. - Store loosely rather than tightly compressed long-term. - Re-seal seams every 1-2 seasons of heavy use.

TrailBlaze 65L Backpack - User Manual Model: BP-65 Category: Outdoor MSRP: $189 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 65-liter capacity - adjustable torso length 16-21 inches - aluminum stay frame - rain cover included 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Adjust the torso length using the back panel slider before loading. 2. Loosen all straps before putting the pack on for the first time. 3. Load heavy items close to your back, roughly at shoulder height. 4. Tighten the hip belt first, then shoulder straps, then load lifters. 3. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not exceed the recommended max load of 50 lbs for the frame. - Check zipper stitching before multi-day trips. - Store dry to prevent mildew on the fabric. 4. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 5-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. Lifetime warranty against manufacturing defects; normal wear is not covered. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 5. MAINTENANCE - Hand wash with a mild detergent; never machine wash. - Air dry completely before storing to prevent mold. - Re-waterproof the fabric annually with a spray-on treatment.

TurboBlend Pro Blender - User Manual Model: BL-500 Category: Kitchen MSRP: $129 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 1200W motor - 64oz BPA-free jar - 6 pre-set programs - variable speed dial 1-10 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Wash the jar, lid, and blade assembly before first use. 2. Attach the jar to the base and twist until it locks. 3. Select a pre-set program or use the manual speed dial. 4. Always start on the lowest speed when blending hot liquids. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Jar not locked onto the base. - E2: Motor overload; reduce ingredient volume and retry. - E6: Lid safety sensor not engaged. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (BL-500) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Never insert utensils into the jar while the motor is running. - Do not blend liquids hotter than 104°F without venting the lid. - Keep hands clear of the blade assembly during cleaning. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 3-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. Motor warranty is 5 years; jar and blades covered for 1 year. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Run a cleaning cycle with warm water and a drop of dish soap after each use. - Check the blade gasket for wear every 6 months. - Do not put the base in the dishwasher.

VoltGuard Power Bank - User Manual Model: PB-20K Category: Electronics MSRP: $39 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 20,000mAh capacity - 65W USB-C PD fast charging - 3 output ports - LED charge indicator 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Charge the power bank fully before first use (roughly 2.5 hours at 65W). 2. Connect your device using any of the 3 output ports. 3. Press the button once to check the remaining charge level. 4. Use the USB-C PD port for fastest charging of compatible laptops. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Overcurrent protection triggered; disconnect and reconnect the cable. - E2: Internal temperature too high; allow the unit to cool before continuing. - E3: Short circuit detected on an output port; try a different cable. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (PB-20K) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Do not expose to temperatures above 113°F. - Do not puncture or disassemble the battery housing. - Stop use immediately if the case becomes swollen or discolored. 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty; capacity is expected to degrade to ~80% after 500 charge cycles. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Recharge at least once every 3 months if stored long-term. - Avoid letting the battery fully drain repeatedly. - Keep charging ports free of lint and debris.

WarmWave Space Heater - User Manual Model: SH-150 Category: Home MSRP: $59 1. KEY SPECIFICATIONS - 1500W ceramic heating element - tip-over auto shutoff - overheat protection - covers up to 200 sq ft 2. SETUP AND FIRST USE 1. Place on a flat, stable surface away from furniture and curtains. 2. Plug directly into a wall outlet; avoid extension cords if possible. 3. Select the desired heat level and set the thermostat dial. 4. Allow the unit to cycle off automatically once the room reaches target temperature. 3. TROUBLESHOOTING If an error code appears, refer to the table below: - E1: Tip-over switch activated; the unit shut off because it was knocked over. - E2: Overheat protection triggered; unplug and allow 20 minutes to cool. - E3: Thermostat sensor fault; contact support if error persists. If the issue persists after following these steps, contact customer support with your model number (SH-150) and a description of the problem. 4. SAFETY WARNINGS - Keep at least 3 feet from bedding, curtains, and furniture. - Never leave unattended while children or pets are nearby. - Do not use in bathrooms or other damp areas unless rated for it (this model is not). 5. WARRANTY This product is covered by a 2-year limited warranty from the date of purchase, covering defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. 2-year warranty on the heating element and housing. Proof of purchase is required for all warranty claims. 6. MAINTENANCE - Dust the intake and outlet grilles every 2 weeks during heating season. - Inspect the power cord for fraying before each season of use. - Store in a dry location during the off-season.
